data.title: STOCKS | Kia Motors Shares Plunge 13% After Second-Quarter Results
data.description: Kia Motors shares fell 13% after the company released its second-quarter earnings report. According to Jin10, the stock drop came after the earnings announcement.

data.title: SK Hynix Advances P&T7 Cleanroom Start by About Three Months
data.description:
SK Hynix is accelerating construction of its P&T7 advanced semiconductor packaging plant in Cheongju and has moved up the opening of its first cleanroom by about three months. According to Odaily, industry sources said on July 23 that the company now expects the first cleanroom at P&T7 to begin operating as early as July 2027, compared with its previously announced target of October 2027.

An industry source said SK Hynix recently began bidding for utilities and infrastructure projects for the P&T7 cleanroom ahead of schedule. The source added that the company internally expects the cleanroom opening to be about one quarter earlier than originally planned. Some equipment-related bidding projects did not attract bidders and are being reissued, while all utilities and infrastructure bidding work is expected to be completed by around August at the latest.

data.title: Arrow Finance Details CDP Collateral Support for Yield Assets, Stablecoins, and Tokenized Stocks
data.description:
Foresight News reported that Robinhood ecosystem DeFi protocol Arrow Finance has outlined the collateral types that will be supported when its CDP launches. According to Foresight News, the protocol will support yield-bearing assets, stablecoins, and a broad range of tokenized stocks.

Yield-bearing collateral will include Maple Finance's syrupUSDG, which can continue generating institutional credit yield while being used for borrowing, and Lido's wstETH, which keeps ETH staking exposure while unlocking liquidity. Stablecoin collateral will be USDG.

Arrow Finance said tokenized stocks will open markets for 24 primary and secondary tokenized assets. The primary list includes MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA, SPY, and QQQ, while the secondary list includes AMD, PLTR, COIN, ORCL, BABA, and CRCL.

The protocol said the initial mint cap will remain conservative and will be set separately, with liquidity and minting capacity to expand based on demand. The mainnet launch date and audit updates will be announced later.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Misses Lead Role in SK Hynix ADR Listing
data.description: Morgan Stanley was not selected as the sole lead underwriter for SK Hynix's American depositary receipt listing. According to Jin10, the deal is worth about $26.5 billion, making it the largest IPO by a foreign company in the U.S., and the underwriting fee is about $130 million based on a 0.5% rate. The final underwriting group consists of BofA, Citi, Goldman Sachs, and JPMorgan Chase. Morgan Stanley had previously won assignments for SpaceX and Anthropic and had been viewed as a strong candidate for OpenAI's listing, so the outcome was seen internally as a reputational setback. Industry participants widely believe Morgan Stanley's repeated bearish reports on South Korean semiconductors, including its well-known "memory winter" analysis, played a role in the loss. Several investment bankers, including former Morgan Stanley executives, said more people in the Seoul office now believe the earlier bearish reports may have cost the firm the SK Hynix deal, and that it will need to be more cautious when publishing similar views in the future.

data.title: Japan's 5-Year Government Bond Yield Rises to Record 2.045%
data.description: According to Jin10, Japan's 5-year government bond yield rose to a record 2.045%.

data.title: SpaceX Starship 13th Test Flight Odds Rise on Polymarket
data.description:
Polymarket probabilities for SpaceX Starship’s 13th test flight rose to 77% for July 24 and 83% for July 25, according to PPP prediction market monitoring. According to Odaily, SpaceX previously said the 13th test flight was delayed to July 24 because of weather, but it is unclear whether weather conditions still remain a factor.

SpaceX made its first attempt at the 13th test flight on July 17, but the launch was aborted after some Raptor engines failed to start as expected and the automated launch abort system was triggered. The attempt failed. SpaceX is currently focusing on Starship and has begun declining commercial Falcon rocket launch orders after 2028.

data.title: Aster Launches Aster Vault Beta for Managed Trading Strategies
data.description:
Aster has launched Aster Vault Beta, which lets users participate in trading strategies driven by active management without executing each trade themselves. According to Odaily, users can deposit funds into a Vault, where strategy managers handle trading and users share gains or losses based on their fund allocation.

The company said the Beta phase offers only a limited number of Vaults. Aster said the feature is intended to lower the threshold for users to access active trading strategies through custodial fund management, and the platform will expand the Vault ecosystem based on Beta feedback.

data.title: Bitcoin Realized Losses Hit Record High as Selling Pressure Eases, Analyst Says
data.description:
CryptoQuant analyst Axel Adler said Bitcoin is in a bear-market phase marked by the largest realized losses ever recorded among holders. According to ChainCatcher, the 30-day moving average of realized losses peaked at $1.37 billion on February 20, 2026, above the $1.15 billion cycle high in 2022, while realized losses have since fallen 56.5% to about $597 million.

Adler said loss-driven selling pressure has eased significantly, but demand has not shown a sustained recovery. Realized profits have improved only gradually to $257 million. He said the realized profit 30-day moving average was $257 million as of July 23, down 92.7% from a $3.51 billion peak on December 10, 2024, and 77.7% below the level seen when Bitcoin reached its all-time high of $124,710 on October 6, 2025. The metric hit a low of $191 million on June 14, 2026, before rebounding 34.7%.

Adler said profit-taking pressure has dropped sharply, and the amount of coins sold at a profit remains low for the current cycle. However, he said that does not mean sellers are exhausted or that demand has fully recovered. He added that realized profits rising above $400 million to $500 million would better confirm sustained improvement. The Realized Profit/Loss Ratio has recovered from 0.26 in June to 0.43, but remains below 1. Adler said the current cycle’s absolute realized losses are larger than in 2022, but market stress is still lower on a relative basis. He also said realized losses exceeded realized profits on 190 of the past 291 days since Bitcoin’s all-time high. If the ratio falls back below 0.26 and Bitcoin breaks below the June 30 cycle low of $58,535, he warned that market pressure could intensify further.

data.title: ECB Council Member Kocherl: Action May Be Needed If Inflation Outlook Worsens
data.description: According to Jin10, ECB Governing Council member Kocherl said action would be necessary if the inflation outlook worsened and inflation expectations also deteriorated.

data.title: Anthropic Expands Claude Voice Mode With Opus, Sonnet, and Haiku
data.description:
Anthropic has upgraded Claude’s voice mode, allowing paid users to choose Opus, Sonnet, or Haiku instead of being limited to the faster but less capable Haiku model. According to ChainCatcher, users can switch models during a conversation, return to text at any time, and Claude will default to the model last used in text chat while calling the fastest version in the lineup.

The voice mode can also connect to Gmail, Google Calendar, Google Docs, and Slack, letting users ask Claude to check email, update schedules, or draft replies after requesting authorization. The new version is now available for testing on mobile, desktop, and web. Free users can access only Haiku and one connected tool.

The voice feature currently supports multiple languages including English, Japanese, and Korean, but not Chinese. Anthropic said the update mainly improves model reasoning and tool-use capabilities, and it did not replace the voice model, so naturalness and interruption handling may not change significantly.

data.title: Wisconsin Warns Election Contract Buyers May Lose Voting Eligibility
data.description:
The Wisconsin Elections Commission said residents who buy election-related contracts on Kalshi or Polymarket may lose the right to vote in the same election. According to Odaily, the commission said knowingly voting without eligibility could be a Class I felony.

The commission unanimously approved a legal memo on prediction markets at its July 9 meeting and issued a statewide notice on Tuesday. The restriction applies only to the related election and does not automatically affect a trader’s eligibility in other unrelated elections.

Kalshi election department head Benjamin Freeman said the guidance is “clearly unconstitutional and illegal” and said the company has hundreds of thousands of users in Wisconsin. Kalshi enforcement and legal counsel head Robert DeNault said election markets certified by the CFTC and offered on federally regulated exchanges are legal.

A Polymarket spokesperson said the company will respond to the commission’s claims through the appropriate legal process. Both companies said their products are tradable financial contracts, not gambling.

data.title: RWA Onchain Market Cap Tops $36.8 Billion as Major Financial Firms Expand Tokenized Assets
data.description: The total market value of real-world assets onchain has surpassed $36.8 billion, while the number of holders recorded a monthly net increase of 300,000, a new high. According to PANews, the United Kingdom plans to issue onchain government bonds in 2027, South Korea is advancing stablecoin legislation, and major traditional finance firms including HSBC and BNY Mellon are stepping up their involvement in tokenized assets.

data.title: Iran Says It Used Arash Drones to Strike Bahrain and Jordan Bases
data.description: Iran's military said it carried out drone attacks on facilities at Bahrain's Sheikh Isa Base and Jordan's Azraq Base during the 24th phase of Operation Lightning. According to Odaily, the strikes used Arash drones and targeted fuel storage facilities, equipment warehouses, aircraft hangars, aircraft maintenance facilities, and personnel residences.

data.title: Carney Says Canada May Retaliate Over New U.S. Tariffs
data.description:
According to Lianhe Zaobao citing Reuters, Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said on Thursday, July 23, that Canada would consider retaliation if necessary in response to recent U.S. tariff threats. Speaking in Charlottetown, Prince Edward Island, during a meeting with national and local leaders, Carney said Canada is intensifying trade talks with the United States and would not hesitate to defend its interests if needed. He said the new U.S. tariffs are “unreasonable” and added that if Canada cannot reach an agreement with Washington, all options are on the table, though he did not give details.

U.S. President Donald Trump on July 20 announced a 50% tariff on a range of Canadian goods, set to take effect on August 19. On July 23, the U.S. Trade Representative’s Office said Washington would invoke Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974 to impose new tariffs from July 24, including a 12.5% levy on 46 trading partners, including Singapore, and a 10% levy on 14 trading partners, including Canada, replacing a 10% global temporary import tariff that expires the same day. Oil, natural gas, fertilizer and some food items will be exempt.

data.title: U.S. Education Department Rolls Back 'Disparate Impact' Rules
data.description:
According to Lianhe Zaobao citing Reuters, the U.S. Education Department on Thursday rescinded multiple “disparate impact” rules that for decades barred policies and practices that, while not intended to discriminate, produced disproportionate harm to certain groups. The department said the rules had impeded schools from dealing with minority students’ behavior issues and had forced educational institutions to “consider race and engage in racial balancing.”

Sixty civil rights and education groups said in a statement on Thursday, July 23, that the decision was critical to protecting students from systemic discrimination based on race, color and national origin. Civil rights groups said the rules helped prevent measures such as school closures and discipline policies that appear neutral but could harm or hinder the development of Black, Native American, Latino, Asian and Pacific Islander students. Education Department civil rights assistant secretary Craig Trainor said “equality” should not prevent teachers from maintaining classroom and student safety. U.S. President Donald Trump signed an executive order in April 2025 opposing the “disparate impact” rules and directing federal agencies to stop enforcing them.

data.title: Polymarket Truce Odds Fall for U.S.-Iran 14-Day Ceasefire Event
data.description:
PPP prediction market monitoring showed that on Polymarket, the probability of the “When Will the U.S. and Iran Reach a Two-Week Ceasefire” event stood at 34% for August 14, down 1% on the week. According to Odaily, the probability for August 31 was 51%, down 3% on the week.

Under the settlement rules, the market will resolve to “Yes” if, at any time between the market’s creation and the specified cutoff time of 11:59 p.m. Eastern Time, there is a continuous 14-day period during which the U.S. does not carry out qualifying military action against Iran. Qualifying military action refers to U.S.-initiated airstrikes or surface-to-surface missile strikes directly targeting Iranian territory.

The article said U.S. forces have struck Iranian military targets for 13 consecutive nights and that Iran has rejected U.S. President Donald Trump’s ceasefire proposal. It also said no signs of de-escalation have emerged in the short term. Separately, people familiar with the matter said Trump has grown increasingly frustrated as the war drags on, while his advisers worry that it has pushed up prices, hurt his approval rating, and killed more than a dozen U.S. service members.

data.title: Trader Holds 1.38 Million HYPE Long for 287 Days, Unrealized Profit Reaches $27.34 Million
data.description:
A trader has held a 1.38 million HYPE long position for 287 days, with the position’s current value at $80.71 million and unrealized profit of $27.34 million. According to Odaily, the trader added 4.5 million USDC in margin, including 2 million USDC deposited yesterday and 2.5 million USDC deposited on July 17.

The position was opened at $38.67, the mark price is $58.49, the liquidation price is $52.368, and the leverage is 5x.

data.title: Three Whales Close Long Positions, Realizing $79,800 Losses
data.description:
Hyperinsight monitoring showed that three whales sold their existing long positions during the midday hour, with total trades of about $7.2637 million and realized losses of about $79,800. According to Odaily, one address starting with 0x960 closed 167 Nasdaq 100 longs for about $4.729 million, realizing a loss of about $46,000; an address starting with 0x943 closed 1,248.3 SKHX longs for about $1.502 million, realizing a loss of about $18,000, and then opened a short position in SKHX worth about $225,700; and an address starting with 0x61c closed 3,262 GOOGL longs for about $1.032 million, realizing a loss of about $14,000.

Only the GOOGL trade was confirmed to have been triggered by a $317 stop-loss order, while the others were active loss-cutting closes. At the time of writing, SK Hynix, Google and the Nasdaq 100 were down about 5.8%, 4.2% and 1.9%, respectively.

data.title: Stable Network Transaction Volume Tops 500,000 in 24 Hours
data.description: Stable said its network processed more than 500,000 transactions within 24 hours, while block times remained under 1 second. According to Foresight News, Stable posted the update on X.

data.title: ECB in Good Position Before September Rate Decision, Nagel Says
data.description:
European Central Bank Governing Council member Joachim Nagel said the ECB is in a good position ahead of its September meeting on interest rates, according to Bloomberg.

He said the central bank will have a raft of fresh information to review when it next meets, but did not comment on the next rate move.
